ai_explanation: This is a critical requirement failure. The commitment explicitly requires 'Record satisfactory executed Deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC, grantor(s) to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY, grantee(s), conveying caption premises in MARION county records.' The deed that was actually recorded conveys to different grantees entirely. This means either: (1) the wrong deed was recorded, (2) the transaction has fundamentally changed, or (3) there is a serious documentation error. Without a deed to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY, the title company cannot issue an owner's policy to her as proposed. This must be corrected before closing.
description: Requirement 4 mandates recording a deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY. However, the recorded deed (Instrument #20260174, recorded 01/13/2026) conveys the property to Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson. This is a complete failure to satisfy the requirement and creates a title defect that will prevent issuance of the owner's policy to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY.

title: Requirement 4 Not Satisfied - Wrong Grantee in Recorded Deed

ai_explanation: The presence of two separate mineral-related exceptions creates potential coverage gaps and ambiguity. More importantly, the warranty deed explicitly excepts 'all oil, gas, and other minerals' from the conveyance, meaning the buyer (Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson) is not receiving mineral rights. This is a significant encumbrance that should be clearly disclosed to the proposed insured. The buyer should be aware that mineral rights have been severed and may be subject to future mineral extraction activities.
description: Exceptions 1 and 8 both address mineral interests. Exception 1 covers 'any prior reservation or conveyance of minerals of every kind and character, including oil, gas, sand and gravel.' Exception 8 covers 'oil, gas, coal and other mineral interests.' These overlapping exceptions create ambiguity about what mineral rights are actually reserved or conveyed. The warranty deed on page 27 states 'LESS AND EXCEPT all oil, gas, and other minerals in, on, and under all of the lands herein conveyed,' confirming minerals are severed from the property.

title: Mineral Reservations and Oil/Gas Interests - Duplicate Exceptions

ai_explanation: This is a critical cross-section mismatch between Schedule A (vested title) and the transaction requirements. The commitment requires a deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY, but the actual recorded deed (Instrument #20260174) conveys the property to Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson instead. Additionally, TERRI G. CRAWLEY is listed as the seller but is not the vested owner - she is only a member of the LLC. This creates a chain of title problem and raises questions about authority and proper conveyancing. The commitment explicitly states 'Please advise our customer service department of the circumstances explaining this discrepancy' indicating the title company recognized this as problematic.
description: The commitment shows GARNER TREE FARM, LLC as the vested owner, but TERRI G. CRAWLEY is listed as the seller. This is a fundamental title defect that must be resolved before closing. The deed recorded on 01/13/2026 shows GARNER TREE FARM, LLC as grantor conveying to Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson, not to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY as required by Requirement 4.
